skill.name: fluidsim
skill.description: Guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through FluidSim setup, solver selection, parameter configuration, simulation workflows, MPI runs, and output analysis for computational fluid dynamics.
skill.summary: Plan and analyze FluidSim computational fluid dynamics simulations with solver, parameter, workflow, and output guidance.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: This is a documentation-only FluidSim skill made of markdown files. Static findings for commands, filesystem use, environment access, network access, weak crypto, C2, and reconnaissance are false positives or benign examples. No executable skill code, prompt injection, credential access, or exfiltration behavior was found.

low_findings.title: Markdown Command Examples Require User Review
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged commands are fenced documentation examples for installing FluidSim, launching MPI, or submitting cluster jobs. No skill file executes them automatically.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is plain markdown with fenced bash or Python examples. There are no executable scripts or tool hooks that run these commands.

low_findings.title: File Write Example Is Documentation Only
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The file write appears in a parametric study example that generates cluster job scripts. It is not executed by the skill itself.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The snippet is embedded in a markdown tutorial and writes a user-created job script. No hidden file modification behavior was found.

low_findings.title: Environment Access Is FFT Configuration
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The environment-variable examples select FluidSim FFT backends. They do not read secrets or transmit environment data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The only environment references assign FLUIDSIM_TYPE_FFT values for library selection. No credential names or network sinks are present.

low_findings.title: External URL Is a Documentation Link
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The hardcoded URL points to FluidSim documentation. There is no fetch, callback, telemetry, or download logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL appears as a resource link in markdown. No code path makes a network request.

low_findings.title: Security Keyword Matches Are Scientific False Positives
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. Weak crypto, C2, and reconnaissance labels are keyword collisions with CFD terms and parameter examples, not malicious logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines describe Navier-Stokes simulations, grid dimensions, c2 phase velocity, and forcing parameters. No crypto routines, system probing, or C2 behavior appears nearby.

content.value_statement: Fluid simulation setup can be complex and error prone. This skill guides solver choice, parameter setup, execution, and analysis for FluidSim projects.
actual_capabilities: Explains FluidSim installation options for basic, FFT, and MPI-enabled environments.
Helps select solvers for 2D, 3D, stratified, shallow water, and elastic plate simulations.
Documents parameter groups for domains, viscosity, time stepping, initial fields, forcing, and outputs.
Provides workflows for starting, restarting, and analyzing FluidSim simulations.
Covers MPI runs, cluster submission examples, parametric studies, and performance tuning.
Describes output analysis for HDF5 fields, spatial means, spectra, and visualization tools.
limitations: It is documentation only and does not run simulations by itself.
Users must install FluidSim and required scientific Python dependencies separately.
Cluster examples may need changes for each scheduler and site policy.
It does not validate physical assumptions or numerical convergence for a study.

use_cases.title: Plan a CFD Research Run
use_cases.description: Choose a solver, set resolution, define viscosity, and prepare output settings for a FluidSim experiment.

use_cases.title: Prepare Parallel Simulations
use_cases.description: Review MPI installation, launch commands, cluster submission patterns, and memory-saving configuration before running large simulations.

use_cases.title: Analyze Saved Results
use_cases.description: Load FluidSim outputs, inspect fields, plot spectra, compare parametric runs, and export summary figures.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Solver
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me choose a FluidSim solver for this flow problem: [describe dimensions, physics, and goal]. Explain the tradeoffs.

prompt_templates.title: Build Simulation Parameters
prompt_templates.prompt: Draft FluidSim parameter settings for a [2D or 3D] simulation with resolution [value], viscosity [value], and end time [value].

prompt_templates.title: Plan Output Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an analysis plan for FluidSim outputs from [simulation directory]. Include physical fields, spatial means, spectra, and validation checks.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Parametric Study
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a FluidSim parametric study varying [parameters]. Include output naming, MPI guidance, restart strategy, and comparison metrics.

output_examples.input: User asks which solver to use for a rotating shallow water experiment.
output_examples.output: The skill recommends the one-layer shallow water solver, explains the Coriolis and wave-speed parameters, and lists required outputs.

output_examples.input: User asks how to compare energy spectra across viscosity values.
output_examples.output: The skill outlines a parametric analysis workflow using saved spectra, spatial means, shared labels, and consistent averaging windows.

output_examples.input: User asks how to run a high-resolution 3D case on a cluster.
output_examples.output: The skill summarizes MPI installation, process-count selection, output frequency reduction, checkpointing, and scheduler adaptation steps.

best_practices: Start with a small resolution test before scaling to MPI or long runs.
Save parameters and outputs with clear directory names for each experiment.
Review stability, CFL settings, output periods, and restart files before expensive runs.
anti_patterns: Do not launch large 3D simulations before validating the setup at low resolution.
Do not assume cluster submission examples match your scheduler without edits.
Do not compare spectra from different runs without matching averaging windows.

faq.question: Does this skill execute FluidSim simulations?
faq.answer: No. It provides documentation and workflow guidance. Users run commands in their own environment.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does FluidSim require API keys?
faq.answer: No. The documentation states that FluidSim does not require API keys or authentication tokens.

faq.question: Can it help with MPI simulations?
faq.answer: Yes. It documents MPI installation, launch examples, output behavior, and large-run configuration guidance.

faq.question: Can it analyze existing simulation outputs?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains loading saved simulations and plotting fields, spatial means, spectra, and energy budgets.

faq.question: What should users verify before publishing results?
faq.answer: Users should verify physical assumptions, numerical convergence, resolution sensitivity, and reproducible parameter records.
